# @andes/button

<br />

Buttons used for a variety of actions.

<br />

The UI specs are defined in _[Andes UI](http://andes.adminml.com) > [Button](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/446e04-button/b/26f535)_.

<br />

## How to use

<br />

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash

npm install --save @andes/button

```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component with all the styles in your bundle:

```css
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

Or you can import only the styles that you need in your project like this:

```css
/* Base */
@import '@andes/button/base';

/* By hierarchy */
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/modifier/loud';
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/modifier/quiet';
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/modifier/mute';

/* By size */
/* small */
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/size/small';
/* medium */
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/size/medium';

/* Button with spinner */
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/base';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/indeterminate';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/medium';

/* Dropdown button */
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/modifier/dropdownButton';
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/modifier/sideDropdownButton';
@import '@andes/list/base';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/base';

/* Floating action button */
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/modifier/floatingActionButton';

/* Progress button */
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/modifier/progress';
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/size/medium';

/* Split button */
@import '@andes/button/lib//styles/modifier/splitButton';
@import '@andes/button/lib/styles/modifier/sideSplitButton';
@import '@andes/list/base';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/base';
```

> Always import `@andes/button/base` and then only the styles that you need.

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx

import { Button } from '@andes/button';

```

In case you need to use additional content other than text (for example, an icon), you'll need to import and use a helper component named `ButtonText`:

```jsx

import { Button, ButtonText } from '@andes/button';

```

In case you need to use dropdown button, you'll need to import and use a helper component named `DropdownButton`:

```jsx

import { DropdownButton } from '@andes/button';

```

In case you need to use split button, you'll need to import and use a helper component named `SplitButton`:

```jsx

import { SplitButton } from '@andes/button';

```

In case you need to use progress button, you'll need to import and use a helper component named `ProgressButton`:

```jsx

import { ProgressButton } from '@andes/button';

```

In case you need to use floating action button, you'll need to import and use a helper component named `FloatingActionButton`:

```jsx

import { FloatingActionButton } from '@andes/button';

```

## Public API

### `<Button>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `backgroundType` | Determines the background type where is placed the button. It's used when the button is placed over a colored surface. | `'default', 'complexBackground'` | `'default'` |  |
| `children` | Custom content | `node` |  | ✅ |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `disabled` | Whether it should be disabled | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `fullWidth` | Whether it should be full width and use all of the available width | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `hierarchy` | Style of the button | `string: 'loud', 'quiet', 'mute'` | `'loud'` |  |
| `href` | Location where to point the button. Needed if used with `type` set to `''`. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `loading` | Whether it should have a loading state using the progress indicator circular component | `boolean` | `undefined` |  |
| `onClick` | Callback called when the button is clicked | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `size` | Size of the element | `string: 'small', 'medium' or 'large'` | `'large'` |  |
| `srAnnouncement` | <sup>1</sup>Label that describes the Spinner for Screen Reader users when `loading` | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label of the button for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `type` | Type attribute for button native element | `string` | `'button'` |  |

> 1- If you are using a button with a spinner, a srAnnouncement should be provided for accessibility reasons.

<br />

<br />

### `<ButtonText>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content | `node` |  | ✅ |

<br />

### `<DropdownButton>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `dialogSrLabel` | Accessible label for the menu dialog | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `disabled` | Whether the button should be disabled | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `fullWidth` | Whether it should be full width and use all of the available width | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `hierarchy` | Style of the button | `string: 'loud', 'quiet'` | `'loud'` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `listContent` | Custom list content | `array<ListConfig>` |  | ✅ |
| `menuWidth` | Width of the menu | `number, string` | `undefined` |  |
| `side` | Menu placement in reference to the button | `string: 'bottom', 'bottom-start', 'bottom-end', 'top', 'top-start', 'top-end', 'left', 'left-start', 'left-end', 'right', 'right-start', 'right-end'` | `'bottom'` |  |
| `size` | Size of the element | `string: 'medium' or 'large'` | `'large'` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label of the button trigger for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |

#### ListConfig

```jsx
{
  href: string,
  onClick: function,
  title: string,
  description: string,
}
```

<br />

### `<SplitButton>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content | `string` |  | ✅ |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `dialogSrLabel` | Accessible label for the menu dialog | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `hierarchy` | Style of the button | `string: 'loud', 'quiet'` | `'loud'` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `listContent` | Custom list content | `array<ListConfig>` |  | ✅ |
| `side` | Side where the menu opens | `string: 'top', 'bottom', 'bottomRight', 'topRight'` | `'bottomRight'` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label of the button trigger for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |

#### ListConfig

```jsx
{
  href: string,
  onClick: function,
  title: string,
}
```

<br />

### `<ProgressButton>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content | `node` |  | ✅ |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `hierarchy` | Style of the button | `string: 'loud', 'quiet'` | `'loud'` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `loading` | Loading flag that shows the button in progress | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `progressLabel` | Custom content when **loading** is active | `node` | `undefined` |  |
| `progressSrAnnouncement` | <sup>1</sup>Sr-only label that allows to notificate changes on the progress for screen reader users. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `timeout` | Timeout in milliseconds to delay the `finished` state. (`it's recommended not to use this prop, unless you know what you are doing`) | `number` | `1000` |  |

> 1 - `progressSrAnnouncement` prop should be used for accessibility reasons in case the `progressLabel` prop is not used.

<br />

### `<FloatingActionButton>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `behavior` | Controls whether the button is expanded or collapsed. | `string: 'expanded', 'collapsed'` | `expanded` |  |
| `className` | Custom class. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `hierarchy` | Style of the button | `string: 'loud', 'quiet'` | `'loud'` |  |
| `icon` | Icon to display next to the text. Can be `img` or `svg`. | `node` |  | ✅ |
| `id` | Component identifier. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `onClick` | Custom callback for click actions. | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `size` | Size of the element. | `string: 'small', 'large'` | `large` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label that describes the button text for Screen Reader users. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `text` | Text inside button. | `string` | `undefined` |  |

<br />

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Button](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/button-button--andes-button).

### Basic button

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-Basic.png" alt="Basic button example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react;
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <Button hierarchy="loud">Sign up</Button>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

### With Icon

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-Icon.png" alt="Button with icon example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Button, ButtonText } from '@andes/button';
import Attach24 from '@andes/icons/Attach24';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <Button>
    <span className="icon-container">
      <Attach24 color="white" />
    </span>
    <ButtonText>Attach document</ButtonText>
  </Button>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';

.andes-button__content .icon-container {
  margin-left: -8px;
  margin-right: 12px;
}
```

### With Spinner

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-Spinner.gif" alt="Spinner Button Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => {
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const handleClick = () => {
    if (loading) return;
    setLoading(true);
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!loading) return;

    const finishTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
      setLoading(false);
    }, 1500);

    return () => clearTimeout(finishTimeout);
  }, [loading]);
  
  return (
    <Button
      loading={loading}
      onClick={handleClick}
      srAnnouncement="Processing..."
    >
      Sign Up
    </Button>
  );
};

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

### Progress Button

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-Progress.gif" alt="Progress Button Example" width="650" />

```jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { ProgressButton } from '@andes/button';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => {
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const handleClick = () => {
    if (loading) return;
    setLoading(true);
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!loading) return;

    const finishTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
      setLoading(false);
    }, 2500);

    return () => clearTimeout(finishTimeout);
  }, [loading]);

  return (
    <ProgressButton
      fullWidth
      loading={loading}
      onClick={handleClick}
      progressLabel="Processing..."
    >
      Sign Up
    </ProgressButton>
  );
};

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

### Dropdown Button

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-Dropdown.gif" alt="Dropdown Button Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { DropdownButton } from '@andes/button';
import './styles.scss';

const options = [
  {
    title: 'Phone',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 1', e),
  },
  {
    title: 'Delivery address',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 2', e),
  },
  {
    title: 'Delivery Status',
    href: 'www.mercadolibre.com.ar',
  },
];

const MyComponent = () => (
  <DropdownButton
    hierarchy="quiet"
    listContent={options}
    menuWidth={200}
    side="bottom-start"
  >
    Edit
  </DropdownButton>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
@import '@andes/list/base';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/base';
```

### Split Button

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-Split.gif" alt="Split Button Example" width="400" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { SplitButton } from '@andes/button';
import './styles.scss';

const options = [
  {
    title: 'Pay one installment',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 1', e),
  },
  {
    title: 'Pay several installments',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 2', e),
  },
  {
    title: 'Add to automatic debiting',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 3', e),
  },
];

const MyComponent = () => (
  <SplitButton listContent={options}>
    Make payment
  </SplitButton>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
@import '@andes/list/base';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/base';
```

### FloatingActionButton

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-FAB.png" alt="Floating action button example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { FloatingActionButton } from '@andes/button';
import Add16  from '@andes/icons/Add16';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <FloatingActionButton text="Add" icon={<Add16 />} />
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases.

<br />

### Using Referenced Component Values

Button allows modifying the following attributes according to its `hierarchy`:

| Attribute                | Description                      |
| ------------------------ | -------------------------------- |
| `fill`                   | Button fill color.               |
| `text`                   | Button text color.               |

Taking this into account, the `customTheme` object has the following structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    button: {
      [hierarchy]: {
        color: {
          fill: {
            [state]: fillColorValue,
          },
          text: {
            [state]: textColorValue,
          },
        },
      },
    },
    fab: {
      container: {
        [hierarchy]: {
          color: {
            fill: fillColorValue,
          },
        },
      },
      [hierarchy]: {
        color: {
          fill: {
            [state]: fillColorValue,
          },
          text: {
            [state]: textColorValue,
          },
          icon: {
            [state]: iconColorValue,
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

Possible `hierarchy` values:

```jsx

hierarchy: loud | quiet | mute

```

Possible `state` values:

```jsx

state: default | hover | active | disabled | progress

```

> For a more detailed view of the structure of this object you can refer to the file `@andes/themes/meli-themes/ml.json`

#### Basic button - Example

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-Reference-Theming.gif" alt="Reference Theming Button Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    button: {
      loud: {
        color: {
          fill: {
            default: {
              base: '#232732',
            },
            hover: {
              base: '#616161',
            },
          },
          text: {
            default: {
              base: '#fff',         
            },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Button>Sign Up</Button>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

#### FloatingActionButton - Example

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-FAB-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming Floating Action Button Example" width="105" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { FloatingActionButton } from '@andes/button';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import Add16 from '@andes/icons/Add16';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    fab: {
      loud: {
        color: {
          fill: {
            default: '#232732',
            hover: '#616161',
          },
          text: {
            default: '#fff',
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <FloatingActionButton text="Add" icon={<Add16 />} />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

#### DropdownButton - Example

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-DropdownButton-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming Dropdown Button Example" width="110" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { DropdownButton } from '@andes/button';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const options = [
  {
    title: 'Phone',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 1', e),
  },
  {
    title: 'Delivery address',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 2', e),
  },
  {
    title: 'Delivery Status',
    href: 'www.mercadolibre.com.ar',
  },
];

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    button: {
      dropdown: {
        loud: {
          color: {
            chevron: {
              default: '#000'
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <DropdownButton
      listContent={options}
      menuWidth={200}
      side="bottom-start"
    >
      Edit
    </DropdownButton>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
@import '@andes/list/base';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/base';
```

#### SplitButton - Example

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-SplitButton-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming Split Button Example" width="182" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { SplitButton } from '@andes/button';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const options = [
  {
    title: 'Pay one installment',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 1', e),
  },
  {
    title: 'Pay several installments',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 2', e),
  },
  {
    title: 'Add to automatic debiting',
    onClick: (e) => console.log('Action 3', e),
  },
];

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    button: {
      split: {
        loud: {
          color: {
            chevron: {
              default: '#000',
            },
            fill: {
              default: '#6bb9f0',
              hover: '#007bbd',
              active: '#005f8b',
            },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <SplitButton listContent={options}>
      Make payment
    </SplitButton>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
@import '@andes/list/base';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/base';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using CSS Properties

The component allows passing CSS properties following this structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    button: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
    fab: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
  },
};
```

> Please note that styles applied in this way will impact all Andes buttons regardless of their variant. Additionally, they have higher precedence than styles applied using referenced component values.

#### Example

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-CSS-Properties-Theming.png" alt="CSS Properties Theming Button Example" width="400" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    button: {
      borderRadius: '15px',
      height: '64px',
      width: '300px',
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Button>Sign Up</Button>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

#### Basic button - data-attributes

| data-attribute                | Description                                                |
| ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `data-andes-button`           | Button tag.                                                |
| `data-andes-button-content`   | Button content.                                            |
| `data-andes-button-hierarchy` | Button hierarchy. Can be: `'loud'`, `'quiet'` or `'mute'`. |
| `data-andes-button-size`      | Button size. Can be: `'small'`, `'medium'` or `'large'`.   |
| `data-andes-button-text`      | Button text tag.                                           |
| `data-andes-state`            | Button tag state. Can be: `'disabled'` or `'loading'`.     |

#### FAB button - data-attributes

| data-attribute                    | Description                                                |
| --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `data-andes-fab-button-container` | Floating action container tag.                             |
| `data-andes-fab-button`           | Floating action button tag.                                |
| `data-andes-fab-button-hierarchy` | Button hierarchy. Can be: `'loud'` or `'quiet'`.           |
| `data-andes-fab-button-size`      | Button size. Can be: `'small'` or `'large'`.               |
| `data-andes-state`                | Floating action container tag. Can be: `'expanded'` or `'collapsed'`. |

#### Dropdown button - data-attributes

| data-attribute               | Description                    |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| `data-andes-dropdown-button` | Dropdown button container tag. |
| `data-andes-state`           | Dropdown button container tag state. Can be: `'expanded'` or `'collapsed'`. |

#### Split button - data-attributes

| data-attribute            | Description                 |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| `data-andes-split-button` | Split button container tag. |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

#### Example

<img src="resources/button/docs/Button-Example-Style-Overrides-Theming.png" alt="Style Overrides Theming Button Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    button: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '&[data-andes-button-hierarchy="loud"]': {
          backgroundColor: '#232732',
          '[data-andes-button-content]': {
            fontSize: '22px',
            fontWeight: 100,
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Button>Sign Up</Button>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.
